import json
# AO Semis - Alcaraz beat de Minaur, Sabalenka beat Jovic
# Who are in semis?
with open('articles/2026/01/27/articles.jsonl') as f:
    for line in f:
        a = json.loads(line)
        title = (a.get('title','') or '').lower()
        content = (a.get('content','') or '').lower()
        if 'australian open' in title and ('semi' in title or 'semifinal' in title):
            print(a.get('title','')[:160])
            # Find semifinal matchup info
            if 'semifinal' in content or 'semi-final' in content:
                for kw in ['semifinal', 'semi-final']:
                    idx = content.find(kw)
                    if idx >= 0:
                        print(content[max(0,idx-100):idx+300])
                        break
            print('---')
" | head -20

python3 -c "
# Trump Commerce Secretaries:
# Obama era: Penny Pritzker (2013-2017), John Bryson (2012), Rebecca Blank (acting 2012-2013)
# December 2012: John Bryson had resigned by then, so it would be acting Commerce Secretary
# Actually Bryson resigned June 2012. Rebecca Blank was acting Jul 2012 - Jun 2013
# But 'Trump Commerce Secretary' is confusing - maybe it means a Trump Commerce Secretary who arranged visit?
# Trump's Commerce Secretaries: Wilbur Ross (2017-2021), Gina Raimondo (Biden)
# Howard Lutnick is Trump's current Commerce Secretary (2025-)
# The question says 'Trump Commerce Secretary' so likely Wilbur Ross or Howard Lutnick
print('Wilbur Ross was Trump Commerce Secretary 2017-2021')
print('Howard Lutnick is current Trump Commerce Secretary 2025-')
print('December 2012 visit to Epstein island - could be either')
"

{"result":"Found 1 relevant article chunk(s):

═══ [1] ═══════════════════════════════════════
HEADLINE: Kendrick Lamar, Lady Gaga, K-pop and more: AP predicts who will win at the 2026 Grammys
SOURCE: www.independent.co.uk
PUBLISHED: 2026-01-23 00:00:00 | DOWNLOADED: 2026-01-23 00:00:00
URL: https://www.independent.co.uk/news/kendrick-lamar-lady-gaga-kpop-bad-bunny-sabrina-carpenter-b2906546.html

Kendrick Lamar, Lady Gaga, K-pop and more: AP predicts who will win at the 2026 Grammys

The competition is fierce. Kendrick Lamar's dominance continues at the 2026 Grammy Awards, where he leads the nominations with nine. He's followed closely by Lady Gaga, Jack Antonoff and Canadian record producer/songwriter Cirkut, who boast seven each. Then it's Sabrina Carpenter, Bad Bunny, Leon Thomas and Serban Ghenea with six. But who will win? Who will make history at the 68th Grammy Awards on Feb. 1? The Associated Press’ Maria Sherman and Jonathan Landrum Jr. break down the close races for Grammy glory. Album of the year
“Debí Tirar Más Fotos,” Bad Bunny; “Swag,” Justin Bieber; “Man’s Best Friend,” Sabrina Carpenter; “Let God Sort Em Out,” Clipse, Pusha T and Malice; “Mayhem,” Lady Gaga; “GNX,” Kendrick Lamar; “Mutt,” Leon Thomas; “Chromakopia,” Tyler, the Creator. SHERMAN: Starting off with the big guns. Last year’s “Cowboy Carter” victory was long overdue for Beyoncé. That, and an evolving Recording Academy electorate, is enough to inspire hope that winners may soon be in sync with the current cultural zeitgeist. But change is slow, and it is most likely Lady Gaga will be awarded. “Mayhem” is a great album, but her victory will be more about narrative — recognizing a game-changing artist with her return-to-form record — instead of who should really win this year. That's Bad Bunny’s “Debí Tirar Más Fotos.” (Let’s not forget all Latin Recording Academy members were invited to join the Recording Academy in 2025, but it is not clear how many can actually vote.) The world would love to see a Spanish-language album win for the very first time — Bad Bunny was once again the most streamed artist, globally, on Spotify last year. There's also “GNX,” which deserves the victory, as well. But the 2025 ceremony felt like Kendrick Lamar's victory lap, and even with three rap albums nominated in the top spot, it's not clear whether voters will give him the recognition he deserves. LANDRUM: I understand the appeal of a breakthrough narrative here, especially with such a globally representative field.
"}
